county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: McLennan County formed 1850 from Milam. County seat is Waco. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Waco. Central Texas county, home to Baylor University. Texas Collection at Baylor University is a major repository. Waco History museum. Texas Ranger Hall of Fame and Museum. Check Milam for pre-1850 records. Significant African American community. Czech heritage in West area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for McLennan County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1850; County Clerk in Waco holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: McLennan County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1850 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: McLennan County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1850. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: McLennan County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1850. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: McLennan County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for McLennan County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: McLennan County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). McLennan County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- McLennan County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for McLennan County from 1850. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: McMullen County formed 1858 from Atascosa, Bexar, Live Oak. County seat is Tilden. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Tilden. South Texas county organized 1877. McMullen County Historical Commission. One of Texas's least-populous counties. Ranching heritage. Check Atascosa, Bexar, Live Oak for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for McMullen County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Tilden holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: McMullen County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1858. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: McMullen County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for McMullen County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- McMullen County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for McMullen County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Medina County formed 1848 from Bexar. County seat is Hondo. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hondo. South Texas county with Alsatian heritage — Castroville settled by Henri Castro's Alsatian colonists (1844). Landmark Inn State Historic Site. Castroville has unique Alsatian architecture and records. Check Bexar for pre-1848 records. St. Louis Catholic Church (Castroville) records from 1844.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Medina County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1848; County Clerk in Hondo holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Medina County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Medina County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Medina County German Church Records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist)
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Medina County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Medina County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Medina County from 1848. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Menard County formed 1858 from Bexar. County seat is Menard. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Menard. Hill Country frontier county organized 1871. Presidio de San Saba (Spanish colonial fort, 1757) ruins. Menard County Historical Society. Check Bexar for pre-1858 records. Menard Irrigation Company records. Ranching heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Menard County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Menard holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for Menard County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Menard County Area (Apache and Comanche)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Apache and Comanche in the Menard County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Menard County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Menard County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Midland County formed 1885 from Tom Green. County seat is Midland. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Midland. Permian Basin oil county organized 1885. Permian Basin Petroleum Museum is the major oil industry repository. Midland County Historical Museum. Haley Memorial Library (J. Evetts Haley collections — ranching history). Check Tom Green for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Midland County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1885; County Clerk in Midland holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Milam County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Milam). County seat is Cameron. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Cameron. Original Texas county. Milam County Historical Commission. Ben Milam heritage (Texas Revolution). Check for Republic-era records at TSLAC. Agricultural community. Czech immigrant heritage. Cameron has preserved historic downtown.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Milam County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Cameron holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1836. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Milam County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Milam County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Mitchell County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Colorado City. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Colorado City. West Texas county organized 1881. Colorado City Historical Museum. Colorado City was the original 'Queen City of the West Texas plains.' Oil discovery (1920s). Check Bexar for pre-organization records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Montague County formed 1857 from Cooke. County seat is Montague. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Montague. North Texas county. Montague County Historical Commission. Spanish Fort (Wichita Indian village visited by Spanish). Check Cooke for pre-1857 records. Bowie heritage (nearby). Red River border.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Montgomery County formed 1837 from Washington. County seat is Conroe. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Conroe. Southeast Texas county. Heritage Museum of Montgomery County. Montgomery is one of the oldest towns in Texas. Sam Houston's home in Huntsville is nearby. Check Washington for pre-1837 records. Timber and oil heritage. Lake Conroe area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Montgomery County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1837; County Clerk in Conroe holds deed books
